info.title: Companion Context Protocol Care Network Lookup HTTP Adapter
info.summary: Illustrative OpenAPI surface for CCP Care Network Lookup.
info.description: This OpenAPI document is an adapter sketch. JSON Schema remains the canonical CCP contract. HTTP implementations are CCP-compatible only when they preserve the same authorization, minimization, provenance, visibility, actor-boundary, contact-boundary, action-authority, revocation, and omission semantics defined by SPEC.md.

contact.name: Companion Context Protocol maintainers; see MAINTAINERS.md until a public source URL is assigned

servers.description: Illustrative server URL. Implementations should publish their own base URL.

tags.name: Care Network Lookup
tags.description: Purpose-bound lookup for one pet and one subject actor.

post.summary: Look up one actor in a pet's care network
post.description: Evaluates requester identity, pet identity, subject actor identity, requested scopes, declared purpose, active grant, visibility classes, contact permissions, action authority, revocation status, and freshness. Protocol-level denials return a CCP response envelope with `status: denied`; malformed requests and transport authentication failures use ordinary HTTP errors.

200.description: CCP authorization result. The body may be `ok`, `partial`, or `denied` according to the canonical Care Network lookup response schema.

contact_withheld_partial_response.summary: Partial response with contact channel withheld

bearerAuth.description: Illustrative transport authentication. CCP does not define an authentication mechanism.

CCPVersion.name: CCP-Version
CCPVersion.description: Requested CCP draft version. Implementations should document supported versions.

BadRequest.description: Malformed HTTP request or request body that cannot be parsed or validated.

UnsupportedMediaType.description: Request media type is not supported. Use `application/json`.

RateLimited.description: Request was rate limited by the HTTP implementation.

InternalServerError.description: Unexpected implementation error. The response is transport-level and does not replace CCP authorization decisions.
